meta.note: Faithful, extractive summaries; every item links to its original source.

items.headline: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
items.summary: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the…

items.headline: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the…

items.headline: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
items.summary: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

items.headline: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
items.summary: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited…

items.headline: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
items.summary: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

items.headline: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
items.summary: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

items.headline: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
items.summary: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

items.headline: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
items.summary: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

items.headline: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
items.summary: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

items.headline: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
items.summary: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

items.headline: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
items.summary: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

items.headline: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
items.summary: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

items.headline: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
items.summary: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

items.headline: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 after government greenlight — and announces ‘ChatGPT Work’
items.summary: About two weeks after OpenAI's GPT-5.6 was caught up in regulatory drama - rolled out only to government-approved organizations during a "limited preview" period - the company has received the Trump administration's greenlight for a public…

items.headline: Microsoft’s patch Tuesdays are about to get bigger
items.summary: Windows 11 updates could soon include fixes for more security issues at once. Microsoft said in a blog post on Thursday that it's now using AI to "identify potential issues earlier," which means "customers will see a higher volume of…

items.headline: The Case for Nationalizing Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: 6 points and 0 comments on Hacker News.

items.headline: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
items.summary: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

items.headline: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
items.summary: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

items.headline: Meta says its new AI model is ready to compete on coding
items.summary: After reentering the AI race with its first in-house Muse Spark model in April, Meta is now opening up the doors to developers with a new model that can plug into AI coding software with the new Meta Model API. Meta says that Muse Spark…

items.headline: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
items.summary: The popularity of Spotify Wrapped has kicked off a wide range of year-in-review features, on apps from YouTube to Uber - and now, the lookback trend has come to AI. Anthropic on Thursday announced a "reflect" feature for its Claude…

items.headline: Character.AI wants a piece of the microdrama pie
items.summary: Character.AI's plan to become more than just an LLM-powered chatbot platform is going beyond interactive books, comics, and audio dramas. Today, the company announced the debut of c.ai Series - short-form, episodic videos designed to be…

items.headline: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
items.summary: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

items.headline: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
items.summary: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

items.headline: FL Studio 2026 turns its AI chatbot into your assistant engineer
items.summary: Last year, Image Line introduced Gopher for FL Studio, an AI chatbot that was basically a glorified instruction manual. You asked it how to do something, and it would serve up the relevant instructions. It's the kind of thing I actually…
